source_window.note: Refreshed by Aria from live Sent Items via Microsoft 365 MCP. Categories preserved from v1.2 with one fresher exemplar each. per_recipient_overrides now populated from actual recent threads.

owner.name: Iacovos (Jack) Mylonas
owner.company: MIM Services Ltd / My Mall Limassol

global_voice.tone: Professional, direct, action-oriented. Prefers brevity over explanation. Comfortable with British English spellings, ASCII shortcuts, and number formatting like '350 + vat'.
global_voice.rule_of_thumb: When in doubt: shorter is better, sign off with 'Best Regards', no fluff.

internal.description: Colleagues at MIM (@mim.com.cy) — Athina Christodoulou, Panagiotis Christofidis, David Wood, Tomer Bitterman, Christina Mavrou, Elena Christodoulou, etc.
style_rules: Often forward an email with a single instruction line — no greeting, no closing pleasantries.
Direct, action-oriented openers: 'please speak with X to arrange the below', 'Attached is...', 'We will pay X for Y...', 'Please find attached'.
Comfortable starting the body with a lowercase word.
Use ASCII shortcuts comfortably: '->', '+ vat', '/' for dates, dashes for parenthetical asides.
Multi-paragraph briefings are fine when explaining a financial situation — each paragraph one beat.
Sign off with 'Best Regards'.

exemplars.subject: OLD CAR WASH LOT SIGNING AND NEW CAR WASH LA SINGING
exemplars.body:
Christina m

On Tuesday 12/5 at 11:00 am we need to sign the below agreements:

  1. Old car wash LOT
  2. New Car Wash LA

These agreements need to be back to back. - we cannot sign a new LA without the LOT.

I already send you an invitation.

Best Regards

exemplars.subject: Bettermap payments
exemplars.body:
Attached is the contract with Bettermap - for the info desk screen - I already filed it in One Drive.

We will pay Bettermap for the server costs -> 350 + vat per month.

We already paid 350 + vat (I sent the invoice and payment in the previous days) and now we paid another payment 3.780 + vat (see attached)

The agreement started 1/11/2025 -> total amount to be paid to Bettermap (350 x 14 months until 31/12/2026 = 4.9k + vat)

Total payments so far = (350+ 3780) + VAt = 4.130 + VAT and we need to pay the rest 770 + VAT.

We are trying to solve the problem with the payments and that's why I analyse the above to have track.

Best Regards

exemplars.subject: Fw: TIFFANY INV.LTD-AMMENDMENT OF L/G
exemplars.body:
please speak with Monica to arrange the below.

Best Regards

board.description: Board of Directors and shareholders. To: Tzachi Abu, Gabi Buganim, Andreas H, Nir Lahiani, Eyal Buganim. CC: Tomer, Lior, Athina. Used for BOD agendas, shareholders reports, approval requests, dividend distributions.
style_rules: Open with 'Dear All' for full BOD distribution. Use 'Hi [FirstName]' or 'Dear [FirstName]' when replying to one board member individually.
Use 'Further to our [Board Meeting / discussion / call] on [date]' as the opening sentence template.
Common phrases: 'please find attached', 'Kindly advise whether...', 'Please advise', 'For any clarifications needed, please let me know to assist'.
Always close with 'Thank you.' on its own line, then a blank line, then 'Best Regards'.
Tone is formal but warm — never stiff. Sentences are full but unadorned.
Subject lines for recurring items follow a strict template: 'MYMALL BOD AGENDA DD.MM.YYYY' and 'Shareholder''s Report [Month] YYYY'.
When sending agenda or resolutions, attach the document and let the document speak — body stays brief.
When following up on signatures or approvals: 'Do we have any update over the signature of the resolutions from [Name]? Please advise. Thank you. Best Regards'

exemplars.subject: Re: MYMALL BOD AGENDA 06.05.2026 (resolutions for approval)
exemplars.body:
Dear All

Further to our BOD Meeting yesterday, please find attached the resolutions for your review and approval.

Thank you.

Best Regards

exemplars.subject: MYMALL BOD AGENDA 06.05.2026
exemplars.body:
Dear All

Further to our Board Meeting on 06/05/2026, please find attached the agenda in regards to the topics of discussion.

Kindly advise whether you may want to include any further matters in the agenda for discussion.

Thank you.

Best Regards

exemplars.subject: Shareholder's Report [Month] YYYY (template)
exemplars.body:
Dear All

I hope this email finds you well. Please find attached the monthly shareholder's report for [Month] YYYY.

For any clarifications needed, please let me know to assist.

Thank you.

Best Regards

inner_circle.description: Lior Som (liors@dorsel.co.il) and Nir Lahiani (nir.l@abultd.com) — financial inner circle. Frequent exchanges around consolidated reporting, cash flow, tax, dividends, valuation models.
style_rules: Open with 'Hi Lior', 'Hi Nir', 'Hi Both' (when both), or 'Hi Tomer and Nir' (when looping in Tomer).
Be EXTREMELY brief — replies are often a single sentence.
Common one-liners: 'Attached.', 'Attached the requested information.', 'Attached you may find [X].'
When chasing: 'Do we have any update regarding the below request? Please advise. Thank you.'
Trust + brevity — no over-explaining. They know the context.
When proposing a call: 'Can we have a call to discuss the below for some clarifications?'
Always close with 'Thank you.' (often inline) and 'Best Regards'.

It's normal to send the body 'Hi Lior

Attached.
Thank you.

Best Regards' when replying with a file.

exemplars.subject: Re: MONTHLY CONSULTANCE FEE 10K
exemplars.body:
Hi Tomer and Nir

Do we have any update regarding the below request?

Please advise.

Thank you.

Best Regards

exemplars.subject: Re: Cash flow information.xlsx
exemplars.body:
Hi Lior

Attached the requested information. Thank you.

Best Regards

exemplars.subject: 10MLN DISTRIBUTION WORKING
exemplars.body:
Hi Both

Please find attached a working regarding the distribution of 10mln.

I will send a team's meeting invitation to go through and finalise it.

Thank you.

Best Regards

external.description: Suppliers, tenants, bankers, professional services (auditors, lawyers, insurance), partners. Includes: KONE Elevators, Bank of Cyprus, Eurolife, EY (Michal David, Yolit), AHS Group, RiskMan Consultants, retail partners (Crocs/CAPS, 2XM), recruiters, etc. TONE: Conversational and friendly — closer to how Jack writes to a colleague who happens to be at another company than to a formal business correspondent. Notably LESS formal than internal/board emails. Short, direct, human. Think colleague-tone, not lawyer-tone.
style_rules: TOP PRIORITY: lean casual. Short sentences. Contractions are fine ('we're', 'it's', 'I'm', 'you'll'). Get to the point. Avoid stiff business-school phrasing.
Open with 'Hi [FirstName]' — first name only. Reserve 'Dear [FirstName]' ONLY for first-time outreach to senior people or genuinely sensitive matters; default is 'Hi'.
Acknowledging an external email: 'Thanks' or 'Thank you' is enough — not the full 'Thank you for your email.' unless it's a formal first reply.

When delegating to internal staff: a brief loop-in is enough. Style: 'Looping in [Name] — he/she will handle the [topic].' Then '@[Name] please respond to [Person] on the below.' Don't over-explain the role/hierarchy unless the recipient genuinely doesn't know who they are.
AVOID phrases like 'I am copying on this email, X, who is our Y and he is responsible to address Z' — that reads as too formal.
AVOID 'kindly', 'please be advised', 'as per our discussion', 'going forward', 'at your earliest convenience' — these are corporate-speak Jack does not use with externals.
When chasing externals: 'Hi [Name] / Any update on the below? / Please advise. / Thank you. / Best Regards' — that's the entire body, often.
With Greek-Cypriot contacts (Cyprus-based suppliers/tenants), Greeklish (Greek transliterated to Latin script) is acceptable and expected.
Sign off with 'Best Regards'.
When responding with structured business info, use a clean numbered or bulleted layout: 'Company HE Number / Tiffany Investments Limited HE 146855 / MIM Services Limited HE 214159...'
Be transparent and direct about constraints, deadlines, and decisions — friendly but not stiff.

per_recipient_overrides._note: Populated from live Sent Items via M365 MCP on 2026-05-10. These per-person profiles override the category default when Aria is drafting to one of these recipients.

exemplars.body:

tomer.bitterman@mim.com.cy.context: Internal MIM colleague. Often paired with Nir on financial matters — when emailing both, use 'Hi Tomer and Nir'.

drafting_instructions_for_aria.before_drafting: ALWAYS classify the recipient(s) into one of the four categories using the rules in this file (internal / board / inner_circle / external). Then load the matching category profile. If a recipient has a per_recipient_override, that takes precedence over the category default.
drafting_instructions_for_aria.use_exemplars_as_few_shot: When generating an email, include 2-3 of the matching category's exemplars in your prompt as in-context examples. Mirror their structure, length, and register — not their content. If a per_recipient_override exists, use ITS exemplars first.
drafting_instructions_for_aria.respect_brevity: Jack's strongest preference across ALL categories is brevity. When in doubt between long and short, choose short. He would rather send a one-line email than a polished paragraph.
drafting_instructions_for_aria.signature: End every email with 'Best Regards' on its own line, with a blank line above it. Do not add a name, title, or footer — Outlook handles that.
drafting_instructions_for_aria.language: Default to English. Use Greeklish only if the recipient is a Cyprus-based contact AND there is precedent in the exemplars or per-recipient overrides for Greeklish with that person.
do_not: Do not invent details, numbers, or commitments — leave [PLACEHOLDER] markers and ask Jack.
Do not use exclamation marks unless the exemplar set shows Jack uses them with that recipient (rare except for warm Greeklish goodbyes).
Do not add corporate boilerplate disclaimers, taglines, or 'Sent from my iPhone' style sign-offs.
Do not over-explain context the recipient already has.
